questions.question: Explain the key requirement for function overloading in C++ and why return type alone is insufficient.
expectedPoints: Functions must differ in parameter list (number, type, or order)
Return type alone doesn't work because function calls don't specify return type
Compiler cannot determine which function to call based on return type alone

questions.question: Discuss the process of function overloading resolution in C++, including examples of potential ambiguities and how to avoid them.
expectedPoints: Compiler uses exact match first, then promotions, then conversions
Default arguments can cause ambiguity with overloaded functions
Implicit conversions can create ambiguous situations
Solutions: avoid default arguments in overloaded functions, use explicit casting
Example of ambiguous call with default parameters
